title: Rhinotropis madrensis
taxonomy: Rhinotropis madrensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Polygonaceae. Rhinotropis madrensis is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It belongs to the genus Rhinotropis, which is a group of plants that are often referred to as "smartweed." The plant has a classification of Rhinotropis madrensis (A.Gray) Medley, under the APG III system. It is often classified as a synonym of Polygonum madrense.

intro: Rhinotropis madrensis, also known as Madrean Desert Mahonia, is a perennial shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. Traditionally used in folk medicine, Rhinotropis madrensis has been reported to possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits, including wound healing, digestive issues, and immune system support. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, glycosides, and lignans, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Rhinotropis madrensis is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to treat various health conditions, particularly those related to the skin, digestive system, and immune system.
